Aloe Vera.air_purifying_level: Helps improve indoor air quality and is valued for medicinal and cosmetic use.
Aloe Vera.description: Ghritkumari, commonly known as Aloe vera, is a succulent plant with thick, fleshy, greenish leaves arranged in a rosette pattern. The inner leaf gel contains bioactive compounds and is traditionally used for skin care, burns, wounds, digestion, and immunity support.
Aloe Vera.water_frequency: Water every 10-14 days and let the soil dry between watering
Aloe Vera.sunlight_requirement: Bright indirect sunlight or mild morning sun
Aloe Vera.maintenance_tips: Use fast-draining soil and a pot with drainage holes
Aloe Vera.unique_pro_tip: Rotate the pot every week so the leaves grow evenly toward light
Aloe Vera.soil_type: Well-drained sandy or succulent soil mix
Aloe Vera.common_problem: Soft brown leaves usually mean the plant is being overwatered
Aloe Vera.disease_or_deficiency: Root rot from excess water, brown tips from underwatering, and pale leaves from low light.

Tulsi.air_purifying_level: Aromatic leaves support a fresh indoor environment when grown near sunlight.
Tulsi.description: Tulsi is a sacred aromatic herb widely grown in Indian homes. It is valued for medicinal use, fragrance, and easy propagation in warm conditions.
Tulsi.water_frequency: Water daily in summer or whenever the topsoil feels slightly dry
Tulsi.sunlight_requirement: 4-6 hours of direct sunlight each day
Tulsi.maintenance_tips: Pinch flower buds regularly to keep leaf growth healthy
Tulsi.unique_pro_tip: Morning watering works best because Tulsi prefers consistent moisture without soggy roots
Tulsi.common_problem: Drooping leaves often point to heat stress or inconsistent watering
Tulsi.disease_or_deficiency: Leaf spots, aphids, yellowing from nutrient deficiency, and root rot in soggy soil.

Rose.air_purifying_level: Mostly ornamental; improves garden and balcony aesthetics.
Rose.description: Rose is a flowering ornamental shrub appreciated for colorful blooms, fragrance, and decorative value. It needs good sunlight and airflow for healthy flowering.
Rose.water_frequency: Water deeply 2-3 times per week depending on weather
Rose.sunlight_requirement: At least 6 hours of direct sunlight
Rose.maintenance_tips: Prune dead flowers and maintain airflow around the plant
Rose.unique_pro_tip: Add mulch around the base to lock in moisture and protect roots from heat
Rose.soil_type: Well-drained loamy soil rich in organic matter
Rose.common_problem: Black spots on leaves can appear when airflow is poor or foliage stays wet
Rose.disease_or_deficiency: Black spot, powdery mildew, aphids, iron deficiency yellowing, and fungal leaf spots.

Snake Plant.air_purifying_level: Known for filtering common indoor pollutants and tolerating low-maintenance office spaces.
Snake Plant.description: Snake Plant is a long-living indoor foliage plant with upright, sword-like leaves and a clean structural appearance. It is ideal for homes, offices, and desks because it tolerates neglect and low light.
Snake Plant.water_frequency: Water every 2-3 weeks and reduce watering in winter
Snake Plant.maintenance_tips: Avoid keeping the soil wet for long periods
Snake Plant.unique_pro_tip: Clean the leaves with a soft cloth occasionally to improve photosynthesis
Snake Plant.common_problem: Mushy leaf bases usually mean water is sitting around the roots
Snake Plant.disease_or_deficiency: Root rot, leaf base rot, brown tips from underwatering, and pale leaves from insufficient light.

Money Plant.air_purifying_level: Helps filter indoor pollutants and grows well in offices and homes.
Money Plant.description: Money Plant is a trailing or climbing indoor plant with heart-shaped leaves. It is easy to propagate and commonly grown on moss sticks, shelves, and hanging planters.
Money Plant.water_frequency: Water when the top 1-2 inches of soil become dry
Money Plant.maintenance_tips: Trim long vines to encourage bushier growth
Money Plant.unique_pro_tip: Grow it near a moss stick or support pole to promote larger leaves
Money Plant.common_problem: Yellow leaves often mean too much direct sun or waterlogged soil
Money Plant.disease_or_deficiency: Root rot, yellowing from overwatering, leaf scorch from direct sun, and brown edges from dry air.

Peace Lily.air_purifying_level: Ability to improve indoor air quality by removing common airborne toxins such as formaldehyde, benzene, trichloroethylene, ammonia, and xylene.
Peace Lily.description: Spathiphyllum is a medium-growing evergreen perennial with glossy green leaves and elegant white flowers. Its soft appearance helps create a calm indoor atmosphere and makes it popular for homes and office spaces.
Peace Lily.water_frequency: Moderate watering; keep soil consistently moist but not soggy
Peace Lily.sunlight_requirement: Indirect bright light; tolerates low light
Peace Lily.maintenance_tips: Remove faded blooms and wipe dust from broad leaves
Peace Lily.unique_pro_tip: If the plant droops dramatically, water it and check again after a few hours
Peace Lily.common_problem: Brown leaf tips can come from dry air, salts in water, or irregular watering
Peace Lily.disease_or_deficiency: Brown tips, yellow leaves from overwatering, drooping from underwatering, and root rot in soggy soil.

Spider Plant.air_purifying_level: Useful indoor foliage plant for freshening homes and offices.
Spider Plant.description: Spider Plant is a hardy foliage plant with arching green or variegated leaves and baby plantlets. It is easy to grow and suitable for shelves, tabletops, and hanging baskets.
Spider Plant.water_frequency: Water once a week or when the topsoil dries slightly
Spider Plant.maintenance_tips: Trim brown tips and repot when roots become crowded
Spider Plant.unique_pro_tip: Plant the baby spiderettes in small pots to quickly multiply your collection
Spider Plant.common_problem: Brown tips often come from fluoride in tap water or very dry air
Spider Plant.disease_or_deficiency: Brown tips, root rot, pale leaves from low light, and yellowing from overwatering.

Areca Palm.air_purifying_level: Popular indoor palm that adds humidity and greenery to office spaces.
Areca Palm.description: Areca Palm is a graceful indoor palm with feathery fronds. It works well as a corner plant and benefits from filtered light and steady moisture.
Areca Palm.water_frequency: Water when the top 1-2 inches of soil are dry
Areca Palm.maintenance_tips: Mist occasionally and keep away from harsh afternoon sun
Areca Palm.unique_pro_tip: Group it with other plants to raise humidity around the fronds
Areca Palm.common_problem: Crispy frond tips usually mean low humidity or underwatering
Areca Palm.disease_or_deficiency: Brown tips, spider mites, root rot, and yellowing from nutrient deficiency.

Jade Plant.air_purifying_level: Mostly ornamental; valued as a compact succulent for desks.
Jade Plant.description: Jade Plant is a compact succulent with thick, shiny leaves and woody stems. It is popular as a low-maintenance desk plant and needs careful watering.
Jade Plant.water_frequency: Water every 2-3 weeks after the soil dries out
Jade Plant.sunlight_requirement: Bright light with a few hours of gentle direct sun
Jade Plant.maintenance_tips: Use a heavy pot and fast-draining succulent mix
Jade Plant.unique_pro_tip: Pinch back leggy stems to keep the plant compact and sturdy
Jade Plant.common_problem: Shriveled leaves can mean underwatering, while translucent leaves suggest overwatering
Jade Plant.disease_or_deficiency: Root rot, mealybugs, leaf drop from overwatering, and shriveled leaves from drought stress.

Hibiscus.air_purifying_level: Mostly ornamental; adds bright flowers to balconies and gardens.
Hibiscus.description: Hibiscus is a flowering shrub with large colorful blooms. It needs strong light, regular watering, and feeding during active growth.
Hibiscus.water_frequency: Water regularly to keep the soil evenly moist in warm weather
Hibiscus.sunlight_requirement: At least 6 hours of direct sunlight
Hibiscus.maintenance_tips: Feed during the growing season and remove spent flowers
Hibiscus.unique_pro_tip: Move potted hibiscus gradually into stronger sun to avoid leaf scorch
Hibiscus.common_problem: Bud drop can happen after sudden temperature, light, or watering changes
Hibiscus.disease_or_deficiency: Aphids, whiteflies, yellow leaves from nutrient deficiency, and fungal leaf spots.

Syngonium.air_purifying_level: Ability to filter harmful indoor air pollutants such as xylene, benzene, and formaldehyde.
Syngonium.description: Syngonium is a striking indoor foliage plant known for arrowhead-shaped leaves and easy care requirements. Its compact vining growth makes it suitable for desktops, shelves, and hanging planters, and its calming foliage can support a positive office atmosphere.
Syngonium.water_frequency: Requires less water; water when the topsoil begins to dry
Syngonium.maintenance_tips: Prune long vines to keep compact growth and rotate the pot for even foliage
Syngonium.unique_pro_tip: Use a small moss stick or hanging planter to show its vining growth neatly
Syngonium.common_problem: Yellow leaves usually indicate overwatering or poor drainage
Syngonium.disease_or_deficiency: Root rot, bacterial leaf spot, yellowing from overwatering, and brown edges from dry air.

Aglaonema.air_purifying_level: Ability to filter harmful indoor air pollutants such as benzene, toluene, and formaldehyde.
Aglaonema.description: Aglaonema is a slow-growing evergreen perennial native to Southeast Asia. It grows in a compact bushy form and is a top choice for indoor office environments because of its striking foliage, tolerance to low light, and minimal care requirements.
Aglaonema.water_frequency: Requires low water; let the topsoil dry slightly before watering
Aglaonema.maintenance_tips: Keep leaves clean and avoid cold drafts or direct afternoon sun
Aglaonema.unique_pro_tip: Place it on desks, corners, or reception areas for a modern decorative touch
Aglaonema.soil_type: Well-draining, slightly acidic soil rich in organic matter
Aglaonema.common_problem: Yellowing leaves usually come from overwatering or cold stress
Aglaonema.disease_or_deficiency: Root rot, leaf spot, yellowing from waterlogging, and pale leaves from very low light.

Anthurium.air_purifying_level: Ability to improve indoor air quality by removing common airborne toxins such as formaldehyde, ammonia, toluene, and xylene.
Anthurium.description: Anthurium is a slow to moderate-growing evergreen tropical perennial with glossy heart-shaped foliage and long-lasting colored spathes. Its bold color and lush appearance make it a welcoming ornamental plant for indoor spaces.
Anthurium.water_frequency: Moderate watering; keep soil slightly moist but not waterlogged
Anthurium.sunlight_requirement: Bright indirect light; avoid harsh direct sunlight
Anthurium.maintenance_tips: Use an organic-rich potting mix and keep humidity moderate
Anthurium.unique_pro_tip: Remove faded spathes at the base to encourage a clean, healthy plant shape
Anthurium.common_problem: Brown leaf edges can come from low humidity or salt buildup
Anthurium.disease_or_deficiency: Bacterial blight, root rot, leaf spot, and yellowing from overwatering.

Satavar.air_purifying_level: Primarily medicinal rather than an air-purifying ornamental plant.
Satavar.description: Satavar, also known as Shatavari, is a slender climbing medicinal plant with feathery needle-like branchlets and small fragrant white flowers. It is valued in Ayurvedic medicine for adaptogenic, rejuvenating, and hormone-balancing properties.
Satavar.maintenance_tips: Provide support for climbing stems and avoid waterlogged soil
Satavar.unique_pro_tip: Grow in a deep container or soil bed so roots have room to develop
Satavar.location: Outdoor and indoor near filtered light
Satavar.common_problem: Weak growth can occur when the plant receives too little filtered light
Satavar.disease_or_deficiency: Root rot, leaf yellowing from waterlogging, and weak growth from nutrient deficiency.

Patharchatta.air_purifying_level: Primarily medicinal and succulent ornamental rather than a major air-purifying plant.
Patharchatta.description: Patharchatta, known as Leaf of Life, is a succulent plant with thick, fleshy green leaves. It is traditionally used in remedies for kidney stones, respiratory issues, wounds, burns, and general healing support.
Patharchatta.water_frequency: Requires moderate watering; allow soil to dry slightly between watering
Patharchatta.maintenance_tips: Use a draining pot and trim leggy stems to maintain compact growth
Patharchatta.unique_pro_tip: New plantlets can grow from leaf margins, making propagation simple
Patharchatta.common_problem: Stem rot can occur in soggy soil
Patharchatta.disease_or_deficiency: Root rot, stem rot, mealybugs, and pale stretched growth from insufficient light.
